rationale: A complete WMI persistence triad was established on host-01: an event filter (EV-001) triggers on logon of 'user-02', a CommandLineEventConsumer (A0) executes a base64-encoded PowerShell payload, and the filter-to-consumer binding (EV-002) ties them together — all created within milliseconds. DER-001 confirms the decoded payload disables SSL certificate validation and then downloads and executes arbitrary code via IEX from an internal IP (192.168.0.4:443/GoPro5/black/2018/_rp), a classic cradle pattern consistent with a C2 stager. The consumer name 'WindowsParentalControlMigration' is a masquerading technique to blend with legitimate Windows components. The binding was created under a different user account ('user-01') than the filter/consumer ('corp\user-02'), suggesting possible lateral movement or privilege abuse.
